tool.name: MetaGraph Sequence Search
tool.description: Search petabase-scale biological sequence archives (MetaGraph Online)

param.name: input_fasta
param.label: Input sequences (FASTA)
param.help: DNA, RNA, or protein sequences in FASTA format. Standard limit: 10 sequences / 55,000 characters total. Larger inputs are accepted but may be queued longer.

param.name: sequence_type
param.label: Molecule type
param.help: Choose 'Nucleotide' for DNA/RNA sequences; 'Amino acid' for protein (UniParc only).
param.option: Nucleotide (DNA / RNA)
Amino acid (protein)

param.name: databases
param.label: Database(s) to search
param.help: Select one or more databases. Nucleotide databases require DNA/RNA input; UniParc requires amino acid input.
param.option: RefSeq — NCBI Reference Sequences (32.9 M accessions; DNA · Align · Coordinates)
AllTheBacteria — bacterial WGS assemblies (2.76 M genomes; DNA · Coordinates)
GnomAD — human genomic variation (29 chromosomal segments; DNA · Align)
UHGG Catalog — Unified Human Gut Genome catalogue v1 (4,644 genomes; DNA · Align)
UHGG All — all UHGG non-redundant genomes (287 K genomes; DNA · Align)
SRA MetaGut — human gut metagenome (241 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
Tara Oceans Genomes — ocean metagenomes with coordinates (34 K genomes; DNA · Align · Coordinates)
Tara Oceans Scaffolds — assembled ocean scaffolds (318 M scaffolds; DNA · Align)
MetaSUB — urban microbiome k=41 (4,220 city samples; DNA · Align)
MetaSUB k=19 — urban microbiome k=19 (4,220 city samples; DNA · Align)
SRA Microbe — microbial WGS (446 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Fungi — fungal sequences (122 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Plants — plant sequences (532 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Metazoa — animal sequences excl. human (805 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Human — human raw reads (436 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Mus musculus — mouse raw reads (58 K samples; DNA/RNA · Align)
SRA Logan Contigs — pre-assembled SRA contigs (21.4 M samples; DNA/RNA)
UniParc — UniProt Archive protein sequences (541 M proteins; Amino Acids · Coordinates)

param.name: search_mode
param.label: Search mode
param.help: Exact match is fast and suitable for high-identity queries. Alignment is more sensitive but significantly slower — use for divergent or noisy sequences.
param.option: Exact match (fast · k-mer based)
Alignment (sensitive · seed-chain-extend)

param.name: discovery_threshold
param.label: Discovery threshold
param.help: Minimum fraction of query k-mers that must match a database label (0.0 = most permissive / most hits; 1.0 = strictest / fewest hits). Recommended starting value: 0.5.

param.name: top_labels
param.label: Maximum matched accessions per database
param.help: Caps the number of returned labels per database per query.

param.name: min_exact_match
param.label: Minimum seed coverage (alignment only)
param.help: Minimum fraction of the query that must be covered by exact-match seeds before alignment is attempted. Increase to speed up alignment at the cost of sensitivity.

param.name: max_alternative_alignments
param.label: Maximum alternative alignments (alignment only)
param.help: Number of distinct alignments to return per sequence.

param.name: debug
param.label: Debug mode
param.help: Print raw API JSON to the tool's stderr log. Useful for troubleshooting.

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**MetaGraph Sequence Search** submits your sequences to the `MetaGraph Online <https://metagraph.ethz.ch>`_ service at ETH Zurich and returns matched accessions as a tab-separated table. An internet connection from the Galaxy server is required.
-----
**What is MetaGraph?**
MetaGraph is a petabase-scale full-text search engine for biological sequences. It indexes raw sequencing data from NCBI SRA, ENA, and DDBJ using compact annotated de Bruijn graphs, enabling rapid query of hundreds of millions of samples.
-----
**Inputs**
*FASTA sequences* One or more DNA, RNA, or protein sequences. Standard jobs support up to 10 sequences / 55,000 characters. Longer inputs are accepted but may be queued.
*Molecule type* Select **Nucleotide** for DNA/RNA queries (all databases except UniParc) or **Amino acid** for protein queries (UniParc only).
*Databases* Choose one or more indexes. Key databases:
==================== ====================================================== Database Description ==================== ====================================================== RefSeq NCBI Reference Sequences — curated assemblies (32.9 M) AllTheBacteria All public bacterial WGS assemblies (2.76 M genomes) UHGG / UHGG All Human gut genome catalogues SRA MetaGut Human gut metagenomes (241 K samples) SRA Microbe/Fungi… Taxonomic SRA subsets (DNA/RNA raw reads) Tara Oceans Marine metagenomes / assembled genomes MetaSUB Urban microbiome (city samples worldwide) GnomAD Human genomic variation UniParc Comprehensive protein sequences (551 M, amino acid input) ==================== ======================================================
-----
**Search modes**
*Exact match (fast)* K-mer based search. Each k-mer in the query is looked up exactly in the index. Suitable for high-identity sequences.
*Alignment (sensitive)* Seed–chain–extend alignment to the de Bruijn graph. Captures variants, sequencing errors, and divergent sequences. Significantly slower.
-----
**Parameters**
*Discovery threshold* The minimum fraction of query k-mers that must be found in a database label for that label to appear in the results. Range 0.0–1.0.
- **0.0** — return any label that shares at least one k-mer (most hits) - **0.5** — default; balanced sensitivity / specificity - **1.0** — all k-mers must match (fewest, highest-confidence hits)
*Maximum matched accessions* Hard cap on returned labels per database per query sequence.
-----
**Output columns**
================ ============================================================= Column Description ================ ============================================================= query FASTA sequence name (first word of the ``>`` header line) database MetaGraph database ID searched label Matched sequence/accession ID (e.g. UniParc UPI*, RefSeq NC_*) normalized_score Fraction of query k-mers matched (0.0–1.0); higher = closer match kmer_count Raw count of matching k-mers coordinates K-mer coordinate ranges in the matched sequence (if available) organism Organism name from the enrichment metadata taxid NCBI Taxonomy ID of the matched organism ================ =============================================================
-----
**Notes**
- Results are fetched immediately after the search completes; you do not need to revisit the MetaGraph website. - The tool polls the API every 10 seconds; large queries against multiple databases may take several minutes to hours. - Results on the MetaGraph server expire after 48 hours; this tool downloads them automatically.
-----
**Citation**
If you use MetaGraph in your research, please cite:
Karasikov et al. (2023) *Indexing and analysing nucleotide archives at petabase scale*. Genome Biology. https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-023-02958-x
